actionable_feedback: Add a README sheet in the workbook that enumerates sheet names, key cell ranges, and a short description of the formulas used for normalization, scoring, classification, and validation.
Add explicit 'maxima' cells (one per metric) in the workbook so verifiers can directly reference the denominator used for normalization.

Include a temporary stand-in input cell (and short instructions) for unit-cost-per-mm (or per-area) so the workbook can convert mm reductions to estimated cost when user-provided unit-costs are available; wire that into a budget-expenditure check cell tied to decision_budget_usd.
State an explicit tie-breaker rule in the grower_plan.md and as a short note in the workbook (e.g., how equal scores are resolved).
Expose any multi-record allocation/redistribution steps (if present) as stepwise formulas or an explicit algorithm block in the workbook so reviewers can machine-verify them.

Provide an alternate parseable summary (CSV or JSON) of the key computed cells (maxima, normalized values, weighted scores, classification, recommended cuts) alongside the .xlsx to aid verifiers when openpyxl is unavailable.
Ensure the workbook is saved in a standard .xlsx encoding and, if possible, confirm the verification environment can load openpyxl or include a small parsed preview file to avoid the preview failure.
artifact_specific_comments: grower_plan.md is clear, contains per-record calculations and validation notes and matches recomputed values in actual_outputs.json.
irrigation_deficit_optimizer.xlsx was authored with the expected sheets and formulas per the trace, but preview parsing failed in the verifier environment (openpyxl unavailable).
feedback_summary: Baseline attempt meets the rubric and received full credit. Calculations, ranking, classification, sensitivity check, and recommendation (cap R2 to 30.35 mm; reduce 26.80 mm) are consistent across the grower plan, actual outputs, and trace. The only verification limitation was that the .xlsx preview could not be parsed by the verifier (openpyxl unavailable), so independent, machine-checkable formula inspection was not possible. Cost conversion was intentionally left as 'needs_review' because the packet omitted unit-cost inputs.

notes: Full semantic credit assigned because grower_plan.md, actual_outputs.json, and trace show consistent recomputation and validation.
Cost remains flagged 'needs_review' because no unit-cost input was provided in the embedded packet.

verifier_issues.message: The irrigation_deficit_optimizer.xlsx preview could not be parsed by the preview tool (openpyxl unavailable). The grader relied on grower_plan.md, actual_outputs.json, and the trace to confirm formulas and recomputed scores.
verifier_issues.recommended_action: Include a README sheet and explicit maxima cells in the workbook and/or provide a parseable summary (CSV/JSON). Ensure openpyxl is available in the verification environment or provide an alternate parsed preview.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce a revised attempt (493-revised) that implements the README sheet and explicit maxima cells, adds a unit-cost input temporary stand-in and budget-expenditure check, documents tie-breaker logic, exposes stepwise allocation formulas (if any), and includes a parseable summary (CSV/JSON) of computed results so verifiers can machine-check without depending solely on human-readable narrative.
trace_specific_comments: Execution trace shows independent recomputation of maxima and weighted scores, creation of the OOXML workbook with six sheets, and a post-write validation step comparing cached workbook values to independent calculations. This supports the grader's full-credit decision despite the preview limitation.
